title: streblus taxoides var. microphyllus
intro: Streblus taxoides var. microphyllus, also known as the dwarf or small-leaf breadfruit tree, is a variety of the Streblus taxoides plant, native to the Pacific Islands and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Streblus taxoides var. microphyllus include the treatment of various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a digestive aid and anti-diarrheal agent. Herbal preparations of Streblus taxoides var. microphyllus include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
